#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

workspace_root="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
log_file="$workspace_root/benchmark-verify.jsonl"
started_ms="$(node -e 'process.stdout.write(String(Date.now()))')"
set +e
output="$(bash "/home/akul_medexfinance_com/clasp/compiler/hosted/evals/contract-upgrade-suite/validate.sh" "lead-follow-up-window-upgrade" "$workspace_root" 2>&1)"
status=$?
set -e
ended_ms="$(node -e 'process.stdout.write(String(Date.now()))')"
printf '%s\n' "$output"
node -e 'const fs = require("fs"); fs.appendFileSync(process.argv[1], JSON.stringify({ startedAtMs: Number(process.argv[2]), endedAtMs: Number(process.argv[3]), exitCode: Number(process.argv[4]) }) + "\n");' "$log_file" "$started_ms" "$ended_ms" "$status"
exit "$status"

item.text: The verifier only checks the workspace through the hosted validator, so I’m keeping this narrow. I’ve identified every stale `LeadSummary`/`status` reference; next edit will replace that chain with `LeadFollowUpDigest` and make `main` resolve through `escalationOwner`.

I’m applying the contract change now in the two typed surfaces the brief pointed to: the shared records, then the API/workflow/request functions in `Main.clasp`. After that I’ll run the verifier and only widen the search if it fails.

The contract rewrite is in place. I’m running the required verifier now to catch any missed emitted-artifact expectations, especially around the old schema name disappearing and `main` evaluating through the new request shape.

item.text:
Updated the lead follow-up contract to `LeadFollowUpDigest` and propagated it through the request/state/API chain in [Shared/Lead.clasp](/tmp/nix-shell-1737626-0/clasp-contract-suite-lead-follow-up-window-upgrade-file-hinted-compiler-owned-air-3RN46g/Shared/Lead.clasp) and [Main.clasp](/tmp/nix-shell-1737626-0/clasp-contract-suite-lead-follow-up-window-upgrade-file-hinted-compiler-owned-air-3RN46g/Main.clasp). The downstream lookup now carries `followUpWindowDays` and `escalationOwner`, workflow state stores `digest`, and the summary route returns `LeadFollowUpDigest`.

`main` now resolves to `"sales-manager"` for the high-priority sample input. Verification passed with `bash scripts/verify.sh`.
